task: Write a Python function least_frequent_word(s) returning the lowercase whitespace-separated word with the lowest count, breaking ties alphabetically. An empty string returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The solution correctly identifies the least frequent word in a given string.

results.call: least_frequent_word('c a c a b b')

trace: The function takes a string `s` as input and returns the least frequent word by iterating through each word in the set of words obtained from splitting the lowercase string. It creates an empty frequency dictionary to count the occurrence of each word, then compares the counts to find the word with the lowest frequency (least frequent word). The function handles the edge case of an empty input string by returning an empty string instead. I chose to use a set for faster unique element access and a dictionary to keep track of word frequencies.
